title: Replace Raw Driver Identity With Driver Archetypes Or Embeddings
summary: A recent pit-stop timing thesis found value in driver clustering for strategy analysis.
rationale: A recent pit-stop timing thesis found value in driver clustering for strategy analysis. Your current pipeline uses raw Driver categories and frequencies, but many drivers are sparse or unseen; a behavior-level abstraction should transfer better than memorizing IDs.
implementation_hint: Within each training fold, build target-safe driver profiles from historical rows only: median stint length by compound, average stop count, late-stop tendency, pace fade, position-change aggressiveness, and wet/dry split. Cluster drivers or learn a low-dimensional embedding, then join cluster/embedding features to both train and test; use race-level priors for unseen drivers.
expected_effect: Should regularize high-cardinality driver effects and improve generalization to rare or unseen drivers in test.
risk: Poorly built profiles can leak target information across folds or collapse to noise for low-sample drivers.
